role_title: Technical Program Manager, Model Alignment and Deployment

level_signals: 5+ years of experience in technical program management
Proven ability to lead complex, multi-team programs
Partners with research, safety, product, and UXR leadership on prioritization

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience in technical program management, research operations, or product execution in a fast-moving AI, ML, or research environment.

Deep familiarity with post-training and alignment concepts (supervised fine-tuning, RLHF, AI safety frameworks, LLM evaluation) as well as model deployment/serving, sufficient to engage substantively with both research and infrastructure engineers.
Proven ability to lead complex, multi-team programs in ambiguous, rapidly evolving environments; track record of shipping with quality and speed.
Strong analytical mindset; comfortable working with data and user insights to measure program health, identify trends, and drive decisions.

Exceptional communication skills - able to translate deep technical work into clear narratives for leadership, and to hold detailed technical conversations with engineers across different disciplines.
Obsessive about data integrity, operational rigor, and process quality without letting process slow teams down.
BS in a quantitative, scientific, or technical field; MS or PhD a plus.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Hands-on experience with data pipelines, annotation platforms, ML evaluation tooling, or human-in-the-loop workflows.
Experience managing annotation vendors or external data partners.
Familiarity with distributed training, experiment tracking, or ML infrastructure (Kubernetes, Docker, cloud) and model serving systems.
Prior experience embedded in an AI research team, foundation model lab, or Trust & Safety engineering team.
Direct experience managing AI safety, trust, quality eval, or red-teaming programs.
BS in a quantitative, scientific, or technical field; MS or PhD a plus.

scope.surface: Model Alignment and Deployment — spanning Post-Training, Safety Engineering, Trust & Safety, ML Infra/Model Serving, and User Experience Research (UXR)
primary_metrics_inferred: Data pipeline health and annotation quality
Training run progress and deployment readiness
Model performance and user satisfaction scores
core_responsibilities: Lead planning and execution of cross-functional programs spanning data collection, annotation pipelines, alignment workflows (RLHF, DPO, Constitutional AI), safety guardrails, and model serving
Serve as connective tissue between Post-Training, Safety Engineering, Trust & Safety, ML Infra, UXR, and Product
Develop and maintain custom evaluation frameworks to track model performance and user satisfaction
Drive visibility into data pipeline health, annotation quality, training run progress, and deployment readiness
Partner with research, safety, product, and UXR leadership on prioritization, sequencing, and tradeoffs
Build and refine operational patterns, ontologies, and frameworks used to scale new capability development
Own external partner relationships including annotation vendors, evaluation tooling providers, and data partners

green_flags: High-impact, cross-functional role at a consumer AI unicorn with 20M+ monthly users
Clear articulation of scope and responsibilities across multiple teams
Role sits at intersection of safety, research, and production — broad exposure
Explicit mention of AI safety and alignment as core priorities signals safety-forward culture
red_flags: No location specified — unclear if remote, hybrid, or onsite
No mention of visa sponsorship policy
High buzzword density may obscure actual day-to-day operational expectations
Role spans an unusually broad set of teams (5+ org functions), which could indicate scope creep or unclear ownership
